_changelog: 1.0 (2026-06-06): Initial schema. Replaces Thesis as the primary capital-allocation unit. A Movement is a capital event (open/add/trim/close) attributed directly to one or more catalysts, with a trigger, a conviction, and a point-in-time score_context snapshot for no-look-ahead backtesting. The falsifiable discipline (assumptions + invalidation) survives as an OPTIONAL, machine-checkable risk_discipline block (design decision: option 1, attached to the position via its movements). See docs/PLAN_movement_restructure.md.

1.1 (2026-06-07): ADDITIVE — structured eval fields on risk_discipline.invalidation[] (comparator/threshold/consecutive_days/eval_ticker/eval_note) so the planned exit_watcher can evaluate price stops DETERMINISTICALLY instead of parsing free-text English. `condition` stays the human description; `stop_price_level`/`stop_price_ticker` stay the native human-facing pair; the new fields are the machine-checkable pair (threshold in eval_ticker's units, eval_ticker yfinance-resolvable or null ⇒ Claude-checked). All optional, only set for market_data stops. No fields removed. Sell-signal design: docs/DESIGN_sell_signals.md §6. profit_take[] (Family 3) deferred.
title: Movement
description: A single capital movement attributed to catalyst(s). The unit of the track record: catalysts accumulate P&L across the movements attributed to them. Real money, EUR. Derived objects (Position = net of movements; CatalystLedger = P&L by catalyst) are computed, never authored.

$schema.description: Tier-1 document convention: relative pointer to this schema file.

id.description: Human-readable slug: mov_YYYYMMDD_sectorid_keyword. Never a UUID.

executed_at.description: Execution timestamp. Used for holding-period and point-in-time joins.

action.description: open = new position; add = increase; trim = partial reduce; close = full exit. Covers 'actualizar por un catalizador (add)' and 'abrir porque me lo pensé mejor (open)'.

sector_id.description: Canonical sector_id from sector_taxonomy.yaml. Free-text sector names never used.

currency.description: Trade currency. P&L is always converted to EUR at executed_at.

amount_eur.description: Signed EUR cash deployed (buy +) or returned (sell, recorded on the trim/close). If null, derived from qty*price (+/- fees) at execution.

qty.description: Units transacted (always positive; sign comes from action).

price.description: Execution price per unit in vehicle.currency.

attribution.description: THE core change vs Thesis: the movement is attributed directly to catalyst(s), not to a thesis. Weights sum to 1.0; the CatalystLedger splits this movement's P&L by weight so no catalyst is double-counted.

catalyst_id.description: Event (cat_*) or structural (struct_*) catalyst id. Must exist in data/catalysts/ or config/structural_catalysts/.

trigger.description: What prompted the movement. reconsideration = 'me lo he pensado mejor' (no new external signal). escalation/contradiction = an existing catalyst strengthened/weakened. stop_hit = an invalidation in risk_discipline fired.

conviction.description: Magnitude reason. Pairs with action: a 'small' escalation add vs a 'high' deliberate open. Drives the expected size relative to conviction_tiers.

score_context.description: POINT-IN-TIME snapshot of what the system 'knew' about this sector at executed_at. The key backtest variable: lets you reconstruct the decision without look-ahead. Captured automatically by movement_logger from the latest score_run for the sector; may be null for movements logged before a run existed.

run_id.description: Score-run joined point-in-time at ingest (as-of executed_at). Null until ingest fills it.

rank.description: Cross-sectional rank of the sector in that run.

risk_discipline.description: OPTIONAL falsifiable discipline (design decision: option 1). Machine-checkable, attached to the position via its opening/adding movements. invalidation_watcher reads these to auto-flag; right_reason_score reads assumption outcomes at close. A small satellite movement may omit this entirely; a core movement should carry it. Mirrors the Thesis assumptions/invalidation_conditions shape for clean migration.

invalidation.description: Pre-committed exit/stop rules. Specific, measurable, checkable.

stop_price_level.description: Human-facing stop level in the condition's NATIVE units (e.g. LME copper $/tonne). Display + audit.

stop_price_ticker.description: Human-facing instrument name in the condition (e.g. COPPER_LME). May not be yfinance-resolvable; see eval_ticker.

comparator.description: Structured eval (schema 1.1): the breach direction. `below` = fires when the series drops under `threshold`; `above` = fires when it rises over it. Set only for machine-checkable market_data stops.

threshold.description: Structured eval (schema 1.1): the level the exit_watcher compares `eval_ticker` against, expressed in EVAL_TICKER's units (which may differ from stop_price_level's native units — e.g. a $/tonne LME stop evaluated via a $/lb COMEX proxy → threshold is the $/lb equivalent). See eval_note.

consecutive_days.description: Structured eval (schema 1.1): the persistence window — the breach must hold for this many consecutive trading-day closes before the stop fires (time-independent, a stateless read of price history). Mirrors the condition's 'for N consecutive days'.

eval_ticker.description: Structured eval (schema 1.1): a yfinance-RESOLVABLE ticker the exit_watcher fetches to check this stop (e.g. EURUSD=X, HG=F). Null ⇒ NOT machine-checkable (no clean feed, e.g. LME inventory) ⇒ surfaced as a Claude-checks-with-WebSearch item instead of auto-evaluated.

eval_note.description: Structured eval (schema 1.1): provenance/caveat for the eval mapping — e.g. the LME/COMEX basis approximation when stop_price_ticker and eval_ticker differ, or the unit conversion applied to threshold.

assumptions.description: What must stay true for the bet to hold. Binary pass/fail from a specific data source.

note.description: Optional free-text rationale (the demoted 'thesis' narrative). Not machine-checked.

migrated_from_thesis_id.description: Set when this movement was lifted from a legacy Thesis during the Phase 1 migration. Audit only.

run_id.description: Pipeline run that contextualized this movement (lineage to reports + sector_snapshot).
